Helper Functions

# =============================================================================
# NORMALITY  (SAP 2.2 / 4 / 7.1)
# Input:  cells_df with columns $vals (list of numeric vectors) and $label
# Output: formatted kable of Shapiro-Wilk results
# =============================================================================

normality_table <- function(cells_df, caption_text = "Shapiro-Wilk Results") {
  purrr::map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(cells_df)), function(i) {
    v <- cells_df$vals[[i]]
    v <- v[!is.na(v)]
    if (length(v) < 3)
      return(tibble(Condition = cells_df$label[i], n = length(v),
                    W = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, Normal = "Too few"))
    if (sd(v) == 0)
      return(tibble(Condition = cells_df$label[i], n = length(v),
                    W = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, Normal = "Identical values"))
    sw <- shapiro.test(v)
    tibble(Condition = cells_df$label[i], n = length(v),
           W = round(sw$statistic, 4), p = round(sw$p.value, 4),
           Normal = ifelse(sw$p.value >= 0.05, "Yes", "No"))
  }) %>%
    kable(caption = caption_text, align = "lrrrr") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                  full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12)
}

# =============================================================================
# DESCRIPTIVE TABLE
# =============================================================================

summary_table <- function(df, group_vars, dv,
                          caption_text = "Descriptive Statistics") {
  df %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(group_vars))) %>%
    summarise(n      = sum(!is.na(.data[[dv]])),
              Median = round(median(.data[[dv]], na.rm = TRUE), 3),
              Mean   = round(mean(.data[[dv]],   na.rm = TRUE), 3),
              SD     = round(sd(.data[[dv]],     na.rm = TRUE), 3),
              IQR    = round(IQR(.data[[dv]],    na.rm = TRUE), 3),
              .groups = "drop") %>%
    kable(caption = caption_text, align = "llrrrrr") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                  full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12)
}

# =============================================================================
# SAFE NON-PARAMETRIC WRAPPERS
# Guard against single-group crashes
# =============================================================================

safe_kruskal <- function(values, groups, label = "") {
  grp    <- droplevels(as.factor(groups))
  n_grps <- sum(tapply(values, grp, function(x) sum(!is.na(x))) > 0)
  if (n_grps < 2) {
    cat("  Skipped - fewer than 2 groups with data:", label, "\n")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  print(kruskal.test(values ~ grp))
}

safe_pairwise_wilcox <- function(values, groups, label = "") {
  grp    <- droplevels(as.factor(groups))
  n_grps <- sum(tapply(values, grp, function(x) sum(!is.na(x))) > 0)
  if (n_grps < 2) {
    cat("  Skipped - fewer than 2 groups with data:", label, "\n")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  print(pairwise.wilcox.test(values, grp,
                              p.adjust.method = "bonferroni", exact = FALSE))
}

# =============================================================================
# SAP 2.1  Aggregate RT to per-participant means per Boundary x Trial Type
# =============================================================================

aggregate_rt <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    group_by(participant_id, boundary_position, stimulus_type) %>%
    summarise(mean_rt = mean(rt, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(stimulus_type = as.character(stimulus_type))
}

# =============================================================================
# SAP 2.3 / 2.4  Two-way RT analysis (parametric preferred, non-parametric fallback)
#
# The SAP asks for a two-way ANOVA (Boundary Position x Trial Type) with Tukey
# HSD post-hoc. We first check whether per-participant means are normal using
# Shapiro-Wilk. If normal -> two-way ANOVA + Tukey HSD (SAP 2.4).
# If non-normal -> Kruskal-Wallis for each main effect + paired Wilcoxon for
# Trial Type + within-type KW as an interaction proxy.
#
# NOTE ON INTERACTION (non-parametric path):
# There is no single non-parametric equivalent of a 2-way ANOVA interaction.
# The within-type KW tests (Boundary within Target, Boundary within Lure) serve
# as an approximate interaction check: if the KW result differs markedly across
# trial types, this suggests a boundary x trial-type interaction.
# For a formal non-parametric interaction test, install the ARTool package and
# uncomment the block at the end of this function.
# =============================================================================

rt_analysis <- function(df, exp_label = "") {
  rt_agg <- aggregate_rt(df)
  cat("\n===", exp_label, ": RT Analysis (SAP 2.3 / 2.4) ===\n")
  cat("N participants:", n_distinct(rt_agg$participant_id), "\n")

  # Normality check on per-participant cell means
  all_vals <- rt_agg$mean_rt[!is.na(rt_agg$mean_rt)]
  normal   <- FALSE
  if (length(all_vals) >= 3 && sd(all_vals) > 0) {
    sw     <- shapiro.test(all_vals)
    normal <- sw$p.value >= 0.05
    cat(sprintf("\nShapiro-Wilk on cell means: W = %.4f, p = %.4f -> %s\n",
                sw$statistic, sw$p.value,
                ifelse(normal, "Normal -> two-way ANOVA", "Non-normal -> KW fallback")))
  }

  if (normal) {
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
    # PARAMETRIC PATH: Two-way ANOVA + Tukey HSD  (SAP 2.3 / 2.4)
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
    cat("\n--- Two-Way ANOVA: RT ~ Boundary Position x Trial Type ---\n")
    rt_wide <- rt_agg %>%
      mutate(boundary_position = factor(boundary_position, levels = c("Non","Pre","Post")),
             stimulus_type     = factor(stimulus_type,     levels = c("Target","Lure")))
    m_aov <- aov(mean_rt ~ boundary_position * stimulus_type +
                   Error(participant_id / (boundary_position * stimulus_type)),
                 data = rt_wide)
    print(summary(m_aov))

    cat("\n--- Tukey HSD: Boundary Position post-hoc (SAP 2.4) ---\n")
    # Tukey on boundary position collapsed across trial type
    m_simple <- aov(mean_rt ~ boundary_position + Error(participant_id / boundary_position),
                    data = rt_wide)
    print(TukeyHSD(aov(mean_rt ~ boundary_position, data = rt_wide)))

  } else {
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
    # NON-PARAMETRIC FALLBACK
    # Main Effect: Boundary Position (KW on per-participant means)
    # Main Effect: Trial Type (paired Wilcoxon, same participants)
    # Interaction proxy: KW within Target, KW within Lure
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------
    cat("\n--- Kruskal-Wallis: Main Effect of Boundary Position ---\n")
    safe_kruskal(rt_agg$mean_rt, rt_agg$boundary_position, "Boundary Position")

    cat("\n--- Bonferroni Pairwise Wilcoxon: Boundary Position (SAP 2.4 fallback) ---\n")
    safe_pairwise_wilcox(rt_agg$mean_rt, rt_agg$boundary_position, "Boundary Position")

    cat("\n--- Paired Wilcoxon: Main Effect of Trial Type (Target vs Lure) ---\n")
    tt_wide <- rt_agg %>%
      group_by(participant_id, stimulus_type) %>%
      summarise(mean_rt = mean(mean_rt, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = stimulus_type, values_from = mean_rt)

    if (!all(c("Target", "Lure") %in% names(tt_wide))) {
      cat("  Skipped: Target and/or Lure column absent.\n")
      cat("  Columns:", paste(names(tt_wide), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    } else {
      tt_wide <- tt_wide %>% filter(!is.na(.data[["Target"]]), !is.na(.data[["Lure"]]))
      cat("  Complete pairs:", nrow(tt_wide), "\n")
      if (nrow(tt_wide) >= 3)
        print(wilcox.test(tt_wide$Target, tt_wide$Lure, paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE))
      else
        cat("  Too few complete pairs.\n")
    }

    cat("\n--- Interaction Proxy: Boundary within Target ---\n")
    df_tgt <- rt_agg %>% filter(stimulus_type == "Target")
    safe_kruskal(df_tgt$mean_rt, df_tgt$boundary_position, "Target boundary")
    safe_pairwise_wilcox(df_tgt$mean_rt, df_tgt$boundary_position, "Target boundary")

    cat("\n--- Interaction Proxy: Boundary within Lure ---\n")
    df_lure <- rt_agg %>% filter(stimulus_type == "Lure")
    safe_kruskal(df_lure$mean_rt, df_lure$boundary_position, "Lure boundary")
    safe_pairwise_wilcox(df_lure$mean_rt, df_lure$boundary_position, "Lure boundary")
  }

  # ============================================================
  # COMMENTED OUT: Aligned Ranks Transformation ANOVA
  # Proper non-parametric 2-way interaction test.
  # Requires: install.packages("ARTool")
  #
  # library(ARTool)
  # rt_art <- rt_agg %>%
  #   mutate(boundary_position = factor(boundary_position),
  #          stimulus_type     = factor(stimulus_type),
  #          participant_id    = factor(participant_id))
  # m_art <- art(mean_rt ~ boundary_position * stimulus_type +
  #                Error(participant_id), data = rt_art)
  # print(anova(m_art))
  # ============================================================
}

# =============================================================================
# SAP 5 / 6  Repeated-measures test (Friedman if non-normal) + Bonferroni post-hoc
#
# SAP 5 asks for RM-ANOVA on: lure_CR, target_hit, REC, LDI
# RM-ANOVA requires normality. If violated -> Friedman test.
# SAP 6: Bonferroni-corrected pairwise comparisons.
# =============================================================================

rm_analysis <- function(df, dv, pid_var = "participant_id",
                         within_var = "boundary_position", label = "") {
  cat("\n---", label, ": RM Analysis (SAP 5 / 6) ---\n")

  # Collapse any duplicate participant x boundary rows to a single mean
  df_agg <- df %>%
    group_by(.data[[pid_var]], .data[[within_var]]) %>%
    summarise(across(all_of(dv), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop")

  complete_pids <- df_agg %>%
    group_by(.data[[pid_var]]) %>%
    summarise(n_lev = n_distinct(.data[[within_var]]), .groups = "drop") %>%
    filter(n_lev == 3) %>% pull(.data[[pid_var]])

  df_c      <- df_agg %>% filter(.data[[pid_var]] %in% complete_pids) %>% droplevels()
  n_kept    <- length(complete_pids)
  n_dropped <- n_distinct(df[[pid_var]]) - n_kept
  cat("  Participants included:", n_kept, "| Dropped (incomplete):", n_dropped, "\n")

  if (n_kept < 3) {
    cat("  Too few complete participants.\n"); return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  wide <- df_c %>%
    select(all_of(c(pid_var, within_var, dv))) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = all_of(within_var), values_from = all_of(dv),
                values_fn = mean)

  # Normality check on the DV values
  all_vals <- df_c[[dv]]
  normal   <- FALSE
  if (length(all_vals[!is.na(all_vals)]) >= 3 && sd(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) > 0) {
    sw     <- shapiro.test(all_vals[!is.na(all_vals)])
    normal <- sw$p.value >= 0.05
    cat(sprintf("  Shapiro-Wilk: W = %.4f, p = %.4f -> %s\n",
                sw$statistic, sw$p.value,
                ifelse(normal, "Normal -> RM-ANOVA", "Non-normal -> Friedman")))
  }

  if (normal) {
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------
    # RM-ANOVA (parametric, SAP 5)
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------
    cat("  Running one-way RM-ANOVA...\n")
    df_c[[within_var]] <- factor(df_c[[within_var]], levels = c("Non","Pre","Post"))
    df_c[[pid_var]]    <- factor(df_c[[pid_var]])
    m_rm <- aov(as.formula(paste(dv, "~", within_var,
                                  "+ Error(", pid_var, "/", within_var, ")")),
                data = df_c)
    print(summary(m_rm))

    # Bonferroni post-hoc via pairwise t-tests (SAP 6)
    cat("\n  Bonferroni pairwise t-tests (SAP 6):\n")
    lvls <- levels(droplevels(as.factor(df_c[[within_var]])))
    results_rm <- map_dfr(combn(lvls, 2, simplify = FALSE), function(pair) {
      x1 <- df_c[[dv]][as.character(df_c[[within_var]]) == pair[1]]
      x2 <- df_c[[dv]][as.character(df_c[[within_var]]) == pair[2]]
      tt <- t.test(x1, x2, paired = FALSE)
      tibble(Comparison = paste(pair[1], "vs", pair[2]),
             t = round(tt$statistic, 3), p_raw = round(tt$p.value, 4))
    }) %>%
      mutate(p_bonferroni = round(p.adjust(p_raw, "bonferroni"), 4),
             Significant  = ifelse(p_bonferroni < 0.05, "Yes", "No"))
    print(kable(results_rm,
                caption = paste("Bonferroni Post-Hoc -", label),
                align = "lrrrc") %>%
            kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                          full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12))

  } else {
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------
    # FRIEDMAN TEST (non-parametric fallback, SAP 5)
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------
    mat <- wide %>% select(-all_of(pid_var)) %>% as.matrix()
    print(friedman.test(mat))

    # Bonferroni pairwise Wilcoxon (SAP 6)
    cat("\n  Bonferroni pairwise Wilcoxon (SAP 6):\n")
    long      <- df_c %>% select(all_of(c(pid_var, within_var, dv)))
    levels_bp <- levels(droplevels(as.factor(df_c[[within_var]])))
    results_fw <- map_dfr(combn(levels_bp, 2, simplify = FALSE), function(pair) {
      x1 <- long[[dv]][as.character(long[[within_var]]) == pair[1]]
      x2 <- long[[dv]][as.character(long[[within_var]]) == pair[2]]
      wt <- wilcox.test(x1, x2, paired = FALSE, exact = FALSE)
      tibble(Comparison = paste(pair[1], "vs", pair[2]),
             W = round(wt$statistic, 2), p_raw = round(wt$p.value, 4))
    }) %>%
      mutate(p_bonferroni = round(p.adjust(p_raw, "bonferroni"), 4),
             Significant  = ifelse(p_bonferroni < 0.05, "Yes", "No"))
    print(kable(results_fw,
                caption = paste("Bonferroni Post-Hoc (Wilcoxon) -", label),
                align = "lrrrc") %>%
            kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                          full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12))
  }
  return(invisible(df_c))
}

# =============================================================================
# SAP 7 / 7.1  Compute difference scores and run normality-adaptive one-sample test
#
# Contrasts: Pre - Non and Post - Non (for lure_CR and target_hit)
# values_fn = mean prevents list-column bug if duplicates exist
# =============================================================================

compute_diff_scores <- function(bp_df) {
  bp_df %>%
    group_by(participant_id, boundary_position) %>%
    summarise(lure_CR    = mean(lure_CR,    na.rm = TRUE),
              target_hit = mean(target_hit, na.rm = TRUE),
              .groups    = "drop") %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from  = boundary_position,
                values_from = c(lure_CR, target_hit)) %>%
    mutate(
      lure_pre_diff    = lure_CR_Pre    - lure_CR_Non,
      lure_post_diff   = lure_CR_Post   - lure_CR_Non,
      target_pre_diff  = target_hit_Pre  - target_hit_Non,
      target_post_diff = target_hit_Post - target_hit_Non
    )
}

one_sample_test_adaptive <- function(x, label = "") {
  cat("\n---", label, "---\n")
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) < 3) {
    cat("  Too few observations (n =", length(x), ").\n")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  sw <- shapiro.test(x)
  cat(sprintf("  Shapiro-Wilk: W = %.4f, p = %.4f -> %s\n",
              sw$statistic, sw$p.value,
              ifelse(sw$p.value >= 0.05,
                     "Normal -> one-sample t-test",
                     "NON-NORMAL -> Wilcoxon signed-rank")))
  if (sw$p.value >= 0.05) print(t.test(x, mu = 0))
  else print(wilcox.test(x, mu = 0, exact = FALSE))
}

# =============================================================================
# SAP 8  Normality-aware paired test: Pre vs Post (within-participant)
# =============================================================================

paired_test_adaptive <- function(df, dv, label = "") {
  cat("\n---", label, ": Paired Pre vs Post (SAP 8) ---\n")
  wide <- df %>%
    group_by(participant_id, boundary_position) %>%
    summarise(val = mean(.data[[dv]], na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    filter(boundary_position %in% c("Pre", "Post")) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = boundary_position, values_from = val) %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[["Pre"]]), !is.na(.data[["Post"]]))
  cat("  Complete pairs:", nrow(wide), "\n")
  if (nrow(wide) < 3) { cat("  Too few complete pairs.\n"); return(invisible(NULL)) }
  diffs <- wide$Pre - wide$Post
  sw    <- shapiro.test(diffs)
  cat(sprintf("  Shapiro-Wilk on differences: W = %.4f, p = %.4f -> %s\n",
              sw$statistic, sw$p.value,
              ifelse(sw$p.value >= 0.05, "Normal -> paired t-test",
                     "NON-NORMAL -> Wilcoxon signed-rank")))
  if (sw$p.value >= 0.05) print(t.test(wide$Pre, wide$Post, paired = TRUE))
  else print(wilcox.test(wide$Pre, wide$Post, paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE))
}

# =============================================================================
# SAP 9  Linear mixed-effects model on trial-level data
#
# DV is binary (0/1) per trial; lmer gives a linear probability model.
# For a fully correct binary analysis use glmer(family = binomial) - see
# commented block inside the function.
# =============================================================================

run_lme_trial <- function(df_labeled, exp_label = "") {
  cat("\n===", exp_label, ": LME Models (SAP 9) ===\n")
  cat("Formula: correct ~ boundary_position + (1 | participant_id)\n\n")

  df_lme <- df_labeled %>%
    mutate(
      lure_correct      = as.integer(stimulus_type == "Lure"   & response_type == "Similar"),
      target_hit        = as.integer(stimulus_type == "Target" & response_type == "Old"),
      boundary_position = factor(boundary_position, levels = c("Non", "Pre", "Post"))
    )

  cat("--- Lure Correct Rejection ---\n")
  df_l <- df_lme %>% filter(as.character(stimulus_type) == "Lure")
  if (n_distinct(df_l$participant_id) >= 3) {
    m_l <- lmer(lure_correct ~ boundary_position + (1 | participant_id),
                data = df_l, REML = TRUE)
    print(summary(m_l))
    cat("\nAnova (Type III):\n"); print(anova(m_l))
  } else cat("  Too few participants.\n")

  cat("\n--- Target Hit Rate ---\n")
  df_t <- df_lme %>% filter(as.character(stimulus_type) == "Target")
  if (n_distinct(df_t$participant_id) >= 3) {
    m_t <- lmer(target_hit ~ boundary_position + (1 | participant_id),
                data = df_t, REML = TRUE)
    print(summary(m_t))
    cat("\nAnova (Type III):\n"); print(anova(m_t))
  } else cat("  Too few participants.\n")

  # ============================================================
  # COMMENTED OUT: logistic mixed model (correct for binary DV)
  # Uncomment if preferred over linear probability model above.
  #
  # m_l_glmer <- glmer(
  #   lure_correct ~ boundary_position + (1 | participant_id),
  #   data   = df_lme %>% filter(stimulus_type == "Lure"),
  #   family = binomial
  # )
  # print(summary(m_l_glmer))
  # m_t_glmer <- glmer(
  #   target_hit ~ boundary_position + (1 | participant_id),
  #   data   = df_lme %>% filter(stimulus_type == "Target"),
  #   family = binomial
  # )
  # print(summary(m_t_glmer))
  # ============================================================
}

# =============================================================================
# SAP 10  Lure similarity bin analysis
# =============================================================================

lure_bin_analysis <- function(test_df, task_df, exp_label = "") {
  cat("\n===", exp_label, ": Lure Similarity Bin Analysis (SAP 10) ===\n\n")

  lure_data <- test_df %>%
    filter(stimulus_type == "Lure") %>%
    left_join(task_df %>%
                select(participant_id, image_name, boundary_position) %>%
                distinct(),
              by = c("participant_id", "image_name")) %>%
    filter(!is.na(boundary_position)) %>%
    mutate(
      sim_bin = case_when(
        str_detect(image_name, "_1[_\\.]|bin1|lure1|sim1|low")  ~ "Low",
        str_detect(image_name, "_2[_\\.]|bin2|lure2|sim2|med")  ~ "Medium",
        str_detect(image_name, "_3[_\\.]|bin3|lure3|sim3|high") ~ "High",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      lure_correct = (response_type == "Similar")
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(sim_bin))

  if (nrow(lure_data) == 0) {
    cat("No similarity bin information found in image names.\n")
    cat("Sample lure image names:\n")
    print(head(test_df$image_name[test_df$stimulus_type == "Lure"], 10))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  cat("Lure trials with bin info:", nrow(lure_data), "\n")
  print(table(lure_data$sim_bin, lure_data$boundary_position))

  lure_agg <- lure_data %>%
    group_by(participant_id, boundary_position, sim_bin) %>%
    summarise(lure_CR = mean(lure_correct, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(sim_bin = factor(sim_bin, levels = c("Low", "Medium", "High")))

  cat("\n--- KW: Boundary Position within each Similarity Bin ---\n")
  for (b in levels(lure_agg$sim_bin)) {
    cat("\nBin:", b, "\n")
    sub <- lure_agg %>% filter(sim_bin == b)
    safe_kruskal(sub$lure_CR, sub$boundary_position, paste("Bin:", b))
  }

  cat("\n--- KW: Similarity Bin within each Boundary Position ---\n")
  for (bp_lv in levels(droplevels(as.factor(lure_agg$boundary_position)))) {
    cat("\nBoundary:", bp_lv, "\n")
    sub <- lure_agg %>% filter(as.character(boundary_position) == bp_lv)
    safe_kruskal(sub$lure_CR, sub$sim_bin, paste("Boundary:", bp_lv))
  }

  sum_df <- lure_agg %>%
    group_by(boundary_position, sim_bin) %>%
    summarise(median_CR = median(lure_CR, na.rm = TRUE),
              q25 = quantile(lure_CR, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
              q75 = quantile(lure_CR, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
              .groups = "drop")

  print(
    ggplot(sum_df, aes(x = boundary_position, y = median_CR,
                       color = sim_bin, group = sim_bin)) +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = q25, ymax = q75, fill = sim_bin),
                  alpha = 0.12, color = NA) +
      geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
      geom_point(size = 4, shape = 21, aes(fill = sim_bin),
                 color = "white", stroke = 1.6) +
      scale_color_manual(values = c(Low = "#4D9DE0", Medium = "#E8A838", High = "#E15554")) +
      scale_fill_manual(values  = c(Low = "#4D9DE0", Medium = "#E8A838", High = "#E15554")) +
      labs(title    = paste(exp_label, ": Lure CR by Boundary x Similarity Bin"),
           subtitle = "Shaded band = IQR | Points = median",
           x = "Boundary Position", y = "Lure Correct Rejection Rate",
           color = "Similarity Bin", fill = "Similarity Bin") +
      theme_study(legend = "right")
  )
  return(invisible(lure_agg))
}

# =============================================================================
# PLOTS
# =============================================================================

# --- Spaghetti + summary plot (best for within-participant repeated measures) ---
# Shows individual participant trajectories (thin grey lines) plus group median
# with IQR as a bold overlay. Clearly communicates both individual variability
# and the group-level trend without hiding the data.

spaghetti_plot <- function(df, x_var, y_var,
                            title = "", subtitle = "",
                            x_lab = x_var, y_lab = y_var,
                            facet_var = NULL) {
  sum_df <- df %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(c(x_var, facet_var)))) %>%
    summarise(median_val = median(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE),
              q25        = quantile(.data[[y_var]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
              q75        = quantile(.data[[y_var]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
              .groups    = "drop")

  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[x_var]], y = .data[[y_var]],
                       group = participant_id)) +
    # Individual participant lines
    geom_line(color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_point(color = "grey60", size = 1.5, alpha = 0.5) +
    # Group median + IQR ribbon
    geom_ribbon(data = sum_df,
                aes(y = median_val, ymin = q25, ymax = q75, group = 1),
                fill = "#4D9DE0", alpha = 0.18, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    geom_line(data = sum_df,
              aes(y = median_val, group = 1),
              color = "#2176AE", linewidth = 1.5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    geom_point(data = sum_df,
               aes(y = median_val, fill = .data[[x_var]], group = 1),
               shape = 21, size = 4, color = "white", stroke = 1.8,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = bp_colors) +
    labs(title = title, subtitle = subtitle, x = x_lab, y = y_lab) +
    theme_study()

  if (!is.null(facet_var))
    p <- p + facet_wrap(as.formula(paste("~", facet_var)))
  return(p)
}

# --- Spaghetti for two groups (RT: Target vs Lure) ---
spaghetti_grouped <- function(df, x_var, y_var, group_var,
                               title = "", subtitle = "",
                               x_lab = x_var, y_lab = y_var,
                               colors = c("#4D9DE0", "#E15554")) {
  grp_lvls <- unique(df[[group_var]])
  sum_df <- df %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(c(x_var, group_var)))) %>%
    summarise(median_val = median(.data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE),
              q25        = quantile(.data[[y_var]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
              q75        = quantile(.data[[y_var]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
              .groups    = "drop")

  ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[x_var]], y = .data[[y_var]],
                  group = interaction(participant_id, .data[[group_var]]),
                  color = .data[[group_var]])) +
    geom_line(aes(color = .data[[group_var]]), linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.35) +
    geom_line(data = sum_df,
              aes(y = median_val, group = .data[[group_var]],
                  color = .data[[group_var]]),
              linewidth = 1.6, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    geom_pointrange(data = sum_df,
                    aes(y = median_val, ymin = q25, ymax = q75,
                        color = .data[[group_var]], group = .data[[group_var]]),
                    size = 0.7, fatten = 4, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = setNames(colors, grp_lvls)) +
    labs(title = title, subtitle = subtitle,
         x = x_lab, y = y_lab, color = group_var) +
    theme_study(legend = "right")
}

# --- Difference score plot (violin + box + jitter + mean diamond) ---
diff_score_plot <- function(diff_df, title = "") {
  req <- c("participant_id", "lure_pre_diff", "lure_post_diff",
           "target_pre_diff", "target_post_diff")
  missing <- setdiff(req, names(diff_df))
  if (length(missing) > 0) {
    cat("diff_score_plot: missing columns:", paste(missing, collapse = ", "), "\n")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  plot_df <- diff_df %>%
    select(all_of(req)) %>%
    pivot_longer(-participant_id, names_to = "contrast", values_to = "diff") %>%
    filter(!is.na(diff)) %>%
    mutate(
      Measure   = ifelse(str_detect(contrast, "lure"), "Lure CR", "Target Hit"),
      Condition = factor(ifelse(str_detect(contrast, "pre"), "Pre - Non", "Post - Non"),
                         levels = c("Pre - Non", "Post - Non"))
    )
  if (nrow(plot_df) == 0) {
    cat("diff_score_plot: no data after filtering NAs.\n"); return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Condition, y = diff, fill = Condition)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_violin(alpha = 0.40, trim = FALSE, color = NA) +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, fill = "white", color = "grey30",
                 outlier.shape = NA, linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_jitter(aes(color = Condition), width = 0.07, size = 2.2, alpha = 0.7) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 23, size = 4.5,
                 fill = "white", aes(color = Condition), stroke = 1.8) +
    scale_fill_manual(values  = c("Pre - Non" = "#F5AEAE", "Post - Non" = "#AEECD0")) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Pre - Non" = "#E15554", "Post - Non" = "#3BB273")) +
    facet_wrap(~ Measure, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(title    = title,
         subtitle = "Dashed line = 0 | Diamond = mean | Points = participants",
         x = "Contrast", y = "Difference in Proportion Correct") +
    theme_study()
}

# --- REC / LDI plot ---
rec_ldi_plot <- function(metrics_df, rec_col, ldi_col, title = "") {
  metrics_df %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(all_of(rec_col), all_of(ldi_col)),
                 names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") %>%
    mutate(Metric = recode(Metric, !!rec_col := "REC", !!ldi_col := "LDI"),
           Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("REC", "LDI"))) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Metric, y = Value, fill = Metric, color = Metric)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_violin(alpha = 0.30, trim = FALSE, color = NA) +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.14, fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA, linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.07, size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 23, size = 4.5,
                 fill = "white", stroke = 1.8) +
    scale_fill_manual(values  = c(REC = "#AED4F5", LDI = "#F5AEAE")) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(REC = "#4D9DE0", LDI = "#E15554")) +
    labs(title    = title,
         subtitle = "Dashed line = 0 | Diamond = mean | Points = participants",
         x = "Metric", y = "Score") +
    theme_study()
}

# --- Q-Q plot ---
qq_plot <- function(x, label = "") {
  tibble(vals = x) %>%
    ggplot(aes(sample = vals)) +
    stat_qq(color = "#4D9DE0", alpha = 0.75, size = 2) +
    stat_qq_line(color = "#E15554", linewidth = 0.9, linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(title = paste("Q-Q:", label), subtitle = "Close to line = normal",
         x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles") +
    theme_study()
}

Experiment 1: Both Item and Task

1.1 Load Task Data

# CSV columns (Both & Item experiments):
#   trials.thisN          -> trial row filter (NA = non-trial rows, excluded)
#   image_path            -> stimulus path; basename used as image_name
#   participant           -> participant ID
#   trials.key_resp_9.rt  -> RT window 1 (0-3 s)
#   trials.key_resp_8.rt  -> RT window 2 (add 3 s offset for true RT)
# Task-only CSVs use bare key_resp_9.rt / key_resp_8.rt (auto-detected below)

task_files_1 <- list.files(
  "Both_item_task/both_data", pattern = "_MST_task_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE
)
cat("Task files found:", length(task_files_1), "\n")
## Task files found: 52
load_task <- function(file_path) {
  df  <- suppressMessages(read_csv(file_path, show_col_types = FALSE))
  pid <- if ("participant" %in% names(df)) {
    as.character(as.integer(df$participant[!is.na(df$participant)][1]))
  } else {
    as.character(as.integer(substr(basename(file_path), 1, 5)))
  }
  rt9_col <- if ("trials.key_resp_9.rt" %in% names(df)) "trials.key_resp_9.rt" else "key_resp_9.rt"
  rt8_col <- if ("trials.key_resp_8.rt" %in% names(df)) "trials.key_resp_8.rt" else "key_resp_8.rt"
  if (!rt9_col %in% names(df)) df[[rt9_col]] <- NA_real_
  if (!rt8_col %in% names(df)) df[[rt8_col]] <- NA_real_

  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(trials.thisN)) %>%
    transmute(
      participant_id    = pid,
      image_name        = basename(image_path),
      trial_number      = trials.thisN + 1,
      rt9               = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[rt9_col]])),
      rt8               = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[rt8_col]])),
      rt = case_when(
        !is.na(rt9) ~ rt9 * 1000,
        !is.na(rt8) ~ (3 + rt8) * 1000,
        TRUE        ~ NA_real_
      ),
      event_position    = ((trial_number - 1) %% 7) + 1,
      boundary_position = factor(
        case_when(
          event_position == 1 ~ "Post",
          event_position == 7 ~ "Pre",
          TRUE                ~ "Non"
        ),
        levels = c("Non", "Pre", "Post")
      )
    )
}

df_both_task <- map_dfr(task_files_1, load_task)
cat("Unique participants:", n_distinct(df_both_task$participant_id), "\n")
## Unique participants: 51
df_both_task %>%
  count(boundary_position, name = "Trial Count") %>%
  kable(caption = "Exp 1: Task Trial Counts by Boundary Position") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12)
Exp 1: Task Trial Counts by Boundary Position
boundary_position Trial Count
Non 10400
Pre 2080
Post 2080

1.2 Load Test Data

# CSV columns:
#   trials.thisN           -> trial row filter
#   image_path             -> stimulus path
#   participant            -> participant ID
#   trials.key_resp_3.keys -> "o" = Old | "s" = Similar | "n" = New
# Stimulus type from image name: ^Foils = Foil | b.jpg = Lure | a.jpg = Target

test_files_1 <- list.files(
  "Both_item_task/both_data", pattern = "_MST_test_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE
)
cat("Test files found:", length(test_files_1), "\n")
## Test files found: 52
load_test <- function(file_path) {
  df <- suppressMessages(read_csv(file_path, show_col_types = FALSE))
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(trials.thisN)) %>%
    transmute(
      participant_id = as.character(as.integer(participant)),
      image_name     = basename(image_path),
      stimulus_type  = case_when(
        str_detect(image_name, "(?i)^foils") ~ "Foil",
        str_detect(image_name, "b\\.jpg$")   ~ "Lure",
        str_detect(image_name, "a\\.jpg$")   ~ "Target",
        TRUE                                  ~ NA_character_
      ),
      response_type = case_when(
        trials.key_resp_3.keys == "o" ~ "Old",
        trials.key_resp_3.keys == "s" ~ "Similar",
        trials.key_resp_3.keys == "n" ~ "New",
        TRUE                          ~ NA_character_
      )
    )
}

df_both_test <- map_dfr(test_files_1, load_test)
cat("Unique participants:", n_distinct(df_both_test$participant_id), "\n")
## Unique participants: 51
cat("Stimulus type counts:\n")
## Stimulus type counts:
print(table(df_both_test$stimulus_type, useNA = "always"))
## 
##   Foil   Lure Target   <NA> 
##   1560   3120   3120      0
cat("In task only:", setdiff(sort(unique(df_both_task$participant_id)),
                              sort(unique(df_both_test$participant_id))), "\n")
## In task only:
cat("In test only:", setdiff(sort(unique(df_both_test$participant_id)),
                              sort(unique(df_both_task$participant_id))), "\n")
## In test only:
df_both_test %>%
  filter(!is.na(stimulus_type), !is.na(response_type)) %>%
  count(stimulus_type, response_type) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = response_type, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  kable(caption = "Exp 1: Test Response Counts by Stimulus Type") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12)
Exp 1: Test Response Counts by Stimulus Type
stimulus_type New Old Similar
Foil 1176 73 311
Lure 714 953 1453
Target 356 1992 772

1.3 Join Task and Test

df_both_labeled <- df_both_task %>%
  left_join(
    df_both_test %>%
      filter(stimulus_type %in% c("Target", "Lure")) %>%
      select(participant_id, image_name, stimulus_type, response_type) %>%
      distinct(),
    by = c("participant_id", "image_name")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(stimulus_type), !is.na(rt)) %>%
  mutate(
    stimulus_type     = factor(stimulus_type, levels = c("Target", "Lure")),
    boundary_position = factor(boundary_position, levels = c("Non", "Pre", "Post")),
    participant_id    = factor(participant_id)
  )

cat("Rows after join:", nrow(df_both_labeled), "\n")
## Rows after join: 3205
cat("Participants:", n_distinct(df_both_labeled$participant_id), "\n")
## Participants: 51
df_both_labeled %>%
  count(boundary_position, stimulus_type) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = stimulus_type, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  kable(caption = "Exp 1: RT Cell Counts (Boundary x Trial Type)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12)
Exp 1: RT Cell Counts (Boundary x Trial Type)
boundary_position Target
Non 1106
Pre 1051
Post 1048

1.4 SAP 2.2 — Normality: Encoding RT

cells_rt_1 <- df_both_labeled %>%
  group_by(boundary_position, stimulus_type) %>%
  summarise(vals = list(rt), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(label = paste(boundary_position, stimulus_type, sep = " | "))
normality_table(cells_rt_1, "Exp 1: Shapiro-Wilk — Encoding RT by Cell")
Exp 1: Shapiro-Wilk — Encoding RT by Cell
Condition n W p Normal
Non &#124; Target 1106 0.7622 0 No
Pre &#124; Target 1051 0.7490 0 No
Post &#124; Target 1048 0.8544 0 No 
p_qq <- wrap_plots(pmap(list(cells_rt_1$vals, cells_rt_1$label),
                function(v, lbl) qq_plot(v[!is.na(v)], lbl)), ncol = 3)
print(p_qq)

ggsave("figures_report/exp1_rt_qq_plots.png", p_qq, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 150)

1.5 SAP 2.3 / 2.4 — RT Analysis

rt_analysis(df_both_labeled, "Experiment 1")
## 
## === Experiment 1 : RT Analysis (SAP 2.3 / 2.4) ===
## N participants: 51 
## 
## Shapiro-Wilk on cell means: W = 0.8449, p = 0.0000 -> Non-normal -> KW fallback
## 
## --- Kruskal-Wallis: Main Effect of Boundary Position ---
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  values by grp
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 14.135, df = 2, p-value = 0.0008522
## 
## 
## --- Bonferroni Pairwise Wilcoxon: Boundary Position (SAP 2.4 fallback) ---
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  values and grp 
## 
##      Non     Pre    
## Pre  0.58939 -      
## Post 0.05957 0.00055
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni 
## 
## --- Paired Wilcoxon: Main Effect of Trial Type (Target vs Lure) ---
##   Skipped: Target and/or Lure column absent.
##   Columns: participant_id, Target 
## 
## --- Interaction Proxy: Boundary within Target ---
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  values by grp
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 14.135, df = 2, p-value = 0.0008522
## 
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  values and grp 
## 
##      Non     Pre    
## Pre  0.58939 -      
## Post 0.05957 0.00055
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni 
## 
## --- Interaction Proxy: Boundary within Lure ---
##   Skipped - fewer than 2 groups with data: Lure boundary 
##   Skipped - fewer than 2 groups with data: Lure boundary

1.6 RT Descriptives and Plot

summary_table(aggregate_rt(df_both_labeled),
              c("boundary_position", "stimulus_type"),
              "mean_rt", "Exp 1: Mean RT per Participant per Cell (ms)")

Non Target 51 1663.591 1722.244 468.537 497.331
Pre Target 51 1528.934 1651.075 544.475 444.810
Post Target 51 1817.356 1942.028 526.660 530.301

#  aggregate_rt(df_both_labeled),
#  x_var = "boundary_position", y_var = "mean_rt", group_var = "stimulus_type",
#  title    = "Exp 1: Encoding RT by Boundary Condition",
#  subtitle = "Thin lines = participants | Bold lines = group median | Range = IQR",
#  x_lab = "Boundary Position", y_lab = "Mean RT per Participant (ms)"

1.7 SAP 3.1 — REC and LDI

mst_1 <- df_both_test %>%
  group_by(participant_id) %>%
  summarise(
    p_old_tgt  = mean(stimulus_type == "Target" & response_type == "Old",     na.rm = TRUE),
    p_old_foil = mean(stimulus_type == "Foil"   & response_type == "Old",     na.rm = TRUE),
    p_sim_lure = mean(stimulus_type == "Lure"   & response_type == "Similar", na.rm = TRUE),
    p_sim_foil = mean(stimulus_type == "Foil"   & response_type == "Similar", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(REC = p_old_tgt - p_old_foil,
         LDI = p_sim_lure - p_sim_foil)

mst_1 %>% select(participant_id, REC, LDI) %>%
  mutate(across(c(REC, LDI), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>%
  write_csv("figures_report/exp1_rec_ldi_participant.csv") %>%
  kable(caption = "Exp 1: Per-Participant REC and LDI") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12)

  write_csv("figures_report/exp1_rec_ldi_group.csv") %>%

  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE, font_size = 12)

p_rec_ldi <- rec_ldi_plot(mst_1, "REC", "LDI", "Exp 1: REC and LDI")
print(p_rec_ldi)

ggsave("figures_report/exp1_rec_ldi_plot.png", p_rec_ldi, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 150)

1.8 SAP 3.2 / 4 — Memory Accuracy by Boundary Condition

bp_1 <- df_both_test %>%
  left_join(df_both_task %>%
              select(participant_id, image_name, boundary_position) %>%
              distinct(),
            by = c("participant_id", "image_name")) %>%
  filter(stimulus_type %in% c("Target", "Lure"), !is.na(boundary_position)) %>%
  mutate(
    boundary_position = factor(boundary_position, levels = c("Non", "Pre", "Post")),
    lure_correct      = (stimulus_type == "Lure"   & response_type == "Similar"),
    target_hit        = (stimulus_type == "Target" & response_type == "Old")
  ) %>%
  group_by(participant_id, boundary_position) %>%
  summarise(lure_CR    = mean(lure_correct, na.rm = TRUE),
            target_hit = mean(target_hit,   na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups    = "drop")

cat("Participants in accuracy data:", n_distinct(bp_1$participant_id), "\n")
## Participants in accuracy data: 51
normality_table(
  bp_1 %>% group_by(boundary_position) %>%
    summarise(vals = list(lure_CR), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(label = paste("Lure CR |", boundary_position)),
  "Exp 1: Shapiro-Wilk — Lure CR by Boundary"
)
Exp 1: Shapiro-Wilk — Lure CR by Boundary
Condition n W p Normal
Lure CR &#124; Non 51 NA NA Identical values
Lure CR &#124; Pre 51 NA NA Identical values
Lure CR &#124; Post 51 NA NA Identical values 
normality_table(
  bp_1 %>% group_by(boundary_position) %>%
    summarise(vals = list(target_hit), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(label = paste("Target Hit |", boundary_position)),
  "Exp 1: Shapiro-Wilk — Target Hit by Boundary"
)

              "Exp 1: Lure Correct Rejection Rate")

              "Exp 1: Target Hit Rate")

boundary_position n Median Mean SD IQR
Non 51 0.65 0.669 0.167 0.25
Pre 51 0.65 0.656 0.146 0.20
Post 51 0.60 0.599 0.162 0.20 
# Spaghetti plots: grey = individual participants, blue = group median + IQR band
# p1a <- spaghetti_plot(bp_1, "boundary_position", "lure_CR",
#                      title    = "Lure Correct Rejection",
#                      subtitle = "Thin = participants | Bold = median + IQR",
#                      x_lab = "Boundary Position", y_lab = "Proportion Correct")
# p1b <- spaghetti_plot(bp_1, "boundary_position", "target_hit",
#                     title    = "Target Hit Rate",
#                      subtitle = "Thin = participants | Bold = median + IQR",
#                      x_lab = "Boundary Position", y_lab = "Proportion Correct")
# (p1a | p1b) +
#  plot_annotation(title = "Exp 1: Memory Accuracy by Boundary Condition")

1.9 SAP 5 / 6 — Repeated-Measures Analysis + Post-Hoc

# SAP 5 requires RM analysis for: lure_CR, target_hit, REC, and LDI
# REC and LDI are participant-level scalars (not broken by boundary) so
# RM analysis across boundary conditions cannot be run for them here.
# They are reported descriptively in section 1.7 above.
# If a boundary-stratified REC/LDI is computed in future, run rm_analysis() on those.

rm_analysis(bp_1, "lure_CR",    label = "Exp 1: Lure CR")
## 
## --- Exp 1: Lure CR : RM Analysis (SAP 5 / 6) ---
##   Participants included: 51 | Dropped (incomplete): 0 
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  mat
## Friedman chi-squared = NaN, df = 2, p-value = NA
## 
## 
##   Bonferroni pairwise Wilcoxon (SAP 6):
## <table class="table table-striped table-hover table-condensed" style="font-size: 12px; width: auto !important; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;">
## <caption style="font-size: initial !important;">Bonferroni Post-Hoc (Wilcoxon) - Exp 1: Lure CR</caption>
##  <thead>
##   <tr>
##    <th style="text-align:left;"> Comparison </th>
##    <th style="text-align:right;"> W </th>
##    <th style="text-align:right;"> p_raw </th>
##    <th style="text-align:right;"> p_bonferroni </th>
##    <th style="text-align:center;"> Significant </th>
##   </tr>
##  </thead>
## <tbody>
##   <tr>
##    <td style="text-align:left;"> Non vs Pre </td>
##    <td style="text-align:right;"> 1300.5 </td>

rm_analysis(bp_1, "target_hit", label = "Exp 1: Target Hit")

1.12 SAP 9 — Linear Mixed-Effects Model

run_lme_trial(df_both_labeled, "Exp 1")
## 
## === Exp 1 : LME Models (SAP 9) ===
## Formula: correct ~ boundary_position + (1 | participant_id)
## 
## --- Lure Correct Rejection ---
##   Too few participants.
## 
## --- Target Hit Rate ---
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: target_hit ~ boundary_position + (1 | participant_id)
##    Data: df_t
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 4293.3
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.8193 -1.1287  0.4928  0.8131  1.3274 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups         Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  participant_id (Intercept) 0.01479  0.1216  
##  Residual                   0.21668  0.4655  
## Number of obs: 3205, groups:  participant_id, 51
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                         Estimate Std. Error         df t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)              0.66491    0.02218   94.49160  29.973   <2e-16 ***
## boundary_positionPre    -0.01105    0.02011 3155.91806  -0.549   0.5829    
## boundary_positionPost   -0.06289    0.02013 3156.03773  -3.124   0.0018 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##              (Intr) bndry_pstnPr
## bndry_pstnPr -0.448             
## bndry_pstnPs -0.448  0.490      
## 
## Anova (Type III):
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                   Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value   Pr(>F)   
## boundary_position 2.3896  1.1948     2 3154.9  5.5141 0.004069 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
